title: Loops and iterable traversal

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications draft for Loops and iterable traversal; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: ECMAScript Language Specification; living specification accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Loops and iterable traversal
sources.claim: JavaScript offers index, iterable, property, and array-method iteration with different keys, values, mutation, and async behavior.

sources.locator: MDN JavaScript Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Loops and iterable traversal
sources.claim: JavaScript offers index, iterable, property, and array-method iteration with different keys, values, mutation, and async behavior.

canonicalExplanation.summary: JavaScript offers index, iterable, property, and array-method iteration with different keys, values, mutation, and async behavior.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: for...of visits array values while an index loop is used when positions are required. Your goal is to implement and debug counted, sentinel and iterable loops. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Values, types and expressions; pf abstraction calls; Conditions and switch. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: JavaScript offers index, iterable, property, and array-method iteration with different keys, values, mutation, and async behavior. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: for...in includes enumerable property names, for...of needs an iterable, and async callbacks in forEach are not awaited collectively. This draft therefore rejects: for...in is the preferred way to iterate array values.

includes: Choose for, while, for-of and collection methods while avoiding inappropriate for-in array traversal.
Implement and debug counted, sentinel and iterable loops.
excludes: for...in includes enumerable property names, for...of needs an iterable, and async callbacks in forEach are not awaited collectively.

outcomes: Implement and debug counted, sentinel and iterable loops.

examples.title: Loops and iterable traversal: concrete case
examples.situation: for...of visits array values while an index loop is used when positions are required.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: for...of visits array values while an index loop is used when positions are required.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Use for...of for iterable values; for...in enumerates property keys and is unsuitable as ordinary array-value traversal.
examples.result: The result follows because Use for...of for iterable values; for...in enumerates property keys and is unsuitable as ordinary array-value traversal.

examples.title: Loops and iterable traversal: boundary case
examples.situation: for...in includes enumerable property names, for...of needs an iterable, and async callbacks in forEach are not awaited collectively.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: for...in includes enumerable property names, for...of needs an iterable, and async callbacks in forEach are not awaited collectively.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: for...in is the preferred way to iterate array values.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Use for...of for iterable values; for...in enumerates property keys and is unsuitable as ordinary array-value traversal.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: for...in is the preferred way to iterate array values.
misconceptions.correction: Use for...of for iterable values; for...in enumerates property keys and is unsuitable as ordinary array-value traversal.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications artifact to explain why this belief fails: for...in is the preferred way to iterate array values.

analogy.example: A coordinated event desk can model Loops and iterable traversal, with values, tasks, callbacks, and external messages changing observable state.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Loops and iterable traversal before using exact platform terms
limitations: Human coordination does not reproduce coercion, lexical scope, microtasks, prototypes, or browser security boundaries.
